hypothesis: A coordinated heterogeneous multi-agent exploration strategy—consisting of multiple LLM agents initialized with diverse random seeds and sampling temperatures and managed by a supervisor that allocates unexplored experimental subspaces—will, within a fixed total reactor time, discover a greater proportion of distinct, predefined ALP behaviors (e.g., self-limiting ALD saturation, ALE etching, D-induced passivation, and co-dosing CVD) than a single-agent exploration by reducing path-dependent trapping in local experimental minima.
